You could add it to this https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/develop/server/src/tests-lib/scala/domino/server/util/TestUtils.scala

comments.author: ldebello-ddl
comments.body: Using that class would be tricky because there is an existing method for creating a project summary, so the import conflict and there are repeated import although are not the same (just same class name - using import alias solve that but at least tricky), there is a method like the one you propose in "WorkspaceTestUtil" I can create a file in the domino/server/util like ProjectTestUtils and move it there and try to use from other project, but not sure if that is class is reachable from all the projects

comments.author: niole

comments.author: ldebello-ddl
comments.body: the audit report shows dataset permission changes, so it is fetching projects associated with dataset, so not sure if using project service will allow fetching that data based on principal, another option is using a servicie principal but also not sure if that can allow the fetching

comments.author: niole
comments.body:
got it. I guess we should ultimately ask product if a principal who isn't allowed to see a project that a dataset is attached to should be allowed to see details about the project.

If product says we shouldn't show that information, then maybe in the case that a user can't see details about the project, we could show a string "redacted".

pr_title: [DOM-55556] - Create prediction_data on user's behalf

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body:
We should only allow the service/superuser account to do the first case here right?
That is, only if the principal is a service account can you specify the datasetCreatorId and look it up.  If I'm just a normal Domino user, then this call *must* use the principal in the request.  Otherwise there's an impersonation risk.

pr_title: DOM-54099 - test refactor - AI gateway - API endpoint test fail

comments.author: ddl-cedricyoung
comments.body:
these are negation tests which will wait the maximum time every time AND may pass if something else goes wrong.
Instead can you turn it into positive tests so that we don't incur the timeout cost every time and fail if something else goes wrong?

comments.author: ddl-abishek
comments.body:
commit c68fe8bb867728da068fa97e6d289ad4fd712158 (HEAD -> DOM-54099, origin/DOM-54099)
Author: ddl-abishek <abishek.subramanian@dominodatalab.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 21 13:48:01 2024 -0400

    checking response dict instead of assertion/response errors

comments.author: ddl-abishek
comments.body: @ddl-cedricyoung fyi

pr_title: DOM-54099 - test refactor - AI gateway - API endpoint test fail

comments.author: ddl-cedricyoung
comments.body:
Since this is combining tests, I think we should give the reader hints for each section verified

```suggestion

     * # Verify embedding
     When I wait to click the button "test_llm_endpoints.py --endpoint_name embeddings_test"
      And I wait to click the tab "Results"
     Then I wait to see the text "dict_keys(['object', 'data', 'model', 'usage'])"

     * # Verify chat
     When I wait to click the button "test_llm_endpoints.py --endpoint_name chat2_test"
      And I wait to click the tab "Results"
     Then I wait to see the text "dict_keys(['id', 'object', 'created', 'model', 'choices', 'usage'])"

     * # Verify access error using chat
     When I wait to click the button "test_llm_endpoints.py --endpoint_name chat_test"
      And I wait to click the tab "Results"
      * # Response error is deliberate because the user does not have permissions to access the chat_test endpoint
     Then I wait to see the text "Response Error 400 Client Error"
      And I wait to see the text "Cannot access endpoint chat_test_{SCENARIO_RUN_ID}"

     * # Verify completion
     When I wait to click the button "test_llm_endpoints.py --endpoint_name completions_test"
      And I wait to click the tab "Results"
     Then I wait to see the text "dict_keys(['id', 'object', 'created', 'model', 'choices', 'usage'])"
```

pr_title: DOM-55601 e2e tests to use a async compliant custom environment instead of DSE

comments.author: ddl-kartik-mathur
comments.body: this can be flaky I think since it's trying to install a package for every test run and depends on upstream, we should rather create a docker image from an image of minimal env and use that image to create environment

comments.author: ddl-julia-stepiko
comments.body:
At the moment I'm reusing already existed steps and practices.
We were doing the same steps for running async models on GPU before, and it was stable.
I will talk to qe-team about it

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body:
I think we should do these steps (pip install etc) in some test somewhere if this is what we're expecting our customers to do.
If we're going to provide a pre-existing image to our customers, then we could test that.  But I think we landed on not doing that and just providing instructions/documentation on how to setup an env for async models.

comments.author: ddl-kartik-mathur
comments.body: I agree we should do some test but depending every night on upstream might be hard and more expensive to maintain I think, we can have a separate test that runs for release qualification. Let's see what @ddl-bcolby and team thinks

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body: hmm, would we do the release qualification test with every cloud release?

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: I'm feeling pressure to close this ticket. Fixing the query requires more testing, and I'm not expert in Mongo, so it would take me some more time. But I agree with you, I think leaving this tech debt will likely not be addressed ever, but at least it's documented.

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body: It this contains organization IDs it shouldn't be named `userIds`

pr_title: [DOM-54527] Fetch API Service Layer

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body: Why is the principal's user ID being used as an argument to set the `organizationIds` query parameter? A user ID is not an organization ID.

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
There was an ask to include user access as well: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ygp8u39n387r4SUMSW1DFLzi19owCUB2ys-NLfJcUnc/edit?disco=AAABHAv1IMw

I'm persisting a list of user ids and organization ids in the database. On query, I check if the persisted list has any ids in common with the requesting user's organizations' ids and their own id.

Is this the right way to do that?
